返回本批次

瘟疫船 (2020)

Plague Ships (2020)

www.afloat.com.au 作者 bryanrasmussen 分数 45 评论 10 2026/04/05 01:33
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 不仅仅是红宝石公主号允许未经检查和受感染的乘客上岸，这种情况已经发生了几个世纪。但 600 年前，一些地方官员对感染危险的认识和主动性比我们最近在新南威尔士州看到的要高。古怪的读者可能会怀疑他只在学校的历史和英语课上听过，但他漫无目的地闲聊……自 1887 年以来，每艘抵达外国港口的船只都必须悬挂黄旗，根据我们的飞行员手册，这意味着“我的船很健康，我需要免费通行证”。因此，1963年，由于导航错误，当我和航海伙伴到达法国瑟堡港而不是英国奥尔德尼港时，我们在客舱清洁装置上挂上了一把用过的黄色掸子。当地的税务员对此感到困惑，而且我们没有护照，他耸耸肩，指着当地一家接受英镑的酒吧。这很奇怪，因为几个世纪以来，黄色一直是与疾病和感染相关的颜色。在中世纪，异教徒必须穿黄色衣服，这被认为是地狱之火的颜色。在鼠疫流行期间，受感染的房屋被涂上黄色油漆，居民被迫穿黄色衣服。丹麦于 1887 年颁布法令，为自己的船只悬挂黄旗，以表明它们是健康的，这一做法在国际上得到了采用。 1347 年 12 月，西西里岛墨西拿的居民困惑地看着来自黑海港口的 12 艘商船极其无能地停靠在港口墙边。然后原因就变得非常清楚了。他们的船员很少还活着。活着的人都是瘦骨嶙峋的骷髅，浑身长满黑色脓疮，渗着血和脓。当局命令这些船只离开港口，但为时已晚。五年内，黑死病将导致欧洲超过 2000 万人死亡。据信，这些船上的感染源源自中东和中国。症状是腹股沟、腋下肿胀，并伴有发烧、疼痛、腹泻和呕吐等副作用。这种空气传播的杆菌导致了令人震惊的三分之一的人口死亡。不仅是人类，农场动物也被感染，这进一步传播了疾病，并在人口减少的情况下带来了粮食短缺。 1377年，杜布罗夫尼克建立了一项制度，根据当时的医疗建议，船舶必须等待30天才能进入港口。然后宗教似乎介入了，引用了《圣经》中所有重大创伤，持续了 40 天 40 夜。所以这成为了常态，因为杜布罗夫尼克当时是威尼斯人，语言是意大利语，你知道我们从中得到了什么词。在威尼斯本身的贸易港口，抵达的疑似携带感染的船只被要求将船长乘船送上岸，前往卫生治安官办公室，在那里他将通过玻璃窗接受采访。即使在那时，他们也意识到感染可以通过空气飞沫传播。船长必须出示装货港的健康证明，证明其船员、乘客和货物没有受到感染。如果有疑问，就会全部送到检疫站40天，并对全船进行熏蒸。拿撒勒圣玛丽亚岛上建立了一家隔离医院。你在关注这里吗？那是在 1423 年，距今将近 600 年前。有趣的是，这位自称是冠状病毒和西方世界其他一切问题的权威（“它会像魔法一样消失”）的人，据报道每天阅读的注意力几乎相当于邮票上的平均字数，却因为其来源而将这种感染称为“武汉病毒”或“中国病毒”。我同意，但让我们用正确的名字来称呼西班牙流感吧。还记得您广泛阅读的总统先生吗？你知道吗，100 年前感染了世界三分之一人口并导致 25 至 5000 万人死亡的病毒？可能不是，福克斯电视台没有。盟军将其称为“西班牙流感”，以关注第一次世界大战后期该中立国家遭受的破坏性。但主要是为了掩盖它正在屠杀世界其他地区数百万人的事实。因此，让我们用正确的名称来称呼它：堪萨斯流感。据堪萨斯历史学会称，第一个记录的病例是芬斯顿营地的一名美国陆军厨师，他因发烧 104 华氏度而住院。它迅速在陆军基地蔓延，一个月内感染了 54,000 名士兵。 1,100 人住院，38 人死亡。当地一名医生向公共卫生部门报告了这一情况，但他和同样被感染的武汉举报人一样遭到了拒绝。幸运的是，他活了下来。因此，他们被装进军舰和货轮（汉弗莱·鲍嘉是其中的一名船员），并被送往第一次世界大战战壕中的淤泥和淤泥。这种致命的 H1N1 甲型流感病毒持续存在两年后变异成为地球上已知的最致命的疾病。不仅仅是第一波。它变成了一种毒性更强、更致命的菌株，在不到一天的时间里就夺去了人们的生命。主要适合25至35岁之间的人，他们被自己的肺液淹死了。由于政府不考虑任何社会距离（例如威尼斯人的做法），这种病毒的破坏被延长了，因为它可能会影响军火工厂的生产并减缓大规模的自由贷款活动。 1918 年 9 月，20 万人在得知流感已经过去后聚集在费城参加自由贷款活动。好吧，也许第一波浪潮已经过去，但两天后，每张医院的床位都被填满了。第三波浪潮在……澳大利亚各地发展，并一路返回欧洲。因此，让我们都听从那些知道自己在说什么的人的建议。今天，这不太可能是世界大国的领导人。
  2. 注册 Afloat 电子新闻 获取最新新闻，让您保持活力
  3. AFLOAT 欢迎读者投稿。故事长度不应超过1600字，理想长度约为1300像素以上。请发送电子邮件至 info@afloat.com.au。照片应该是高分辨率 jpeg 文件。 © 版权所有 2025. AFLOAT Publications Pty. Ltd。本网站上的原创内容和广告均为 AFLOAT Publications Pty Ltd 的版权和专有财产。未经出版商书面许可，不得以任何形式或任何方式复制或传播本出版物的任何部分。本网站表达的评论和观点不一定是出版商或编辑的观点。
  4. AFLOAT 承认整个澳大利亚国家的传统所有者及其与土地、水域和社区的联系。我们向过去、现在和未来的长者致以敬意。
  5. © 版权所有 2026 漂浮。版权所有。

Original Summary

  1. It was not just the Ruby Princess that allowed unchecked and infected passengers ashore, this has been happening for centuries. But 600 years ago, some local officials were more cognisant and proactive towards the dangers of infection than we have seen recently in NSW. Quirky, who readers will suspect only paid attention during the history and English classes at school, rambles on … Since 1887, every ship arriving in a foreign port is required to fly a yellow flag which according to our pilot book means “My ship is healthy and I require free pratique.” So in 1963, by a navigational error, when my sailing mates and I arrived in the French port of Cherbourg instead of the British port of Alderney, we hung up a well used yellow duster from the cabin cleaning gear. The local douanier was bemused by this and our lack of passports, he shrugged it off and pointed to a local bar which took English pounds. This is curious because for centuries, yellow was the colour associated with disease and infection. In the Middle Ages, heretics had to wear yellow clothing, this being considered the colour of hell fire. During plague epidemics, infected houses were daubed with yellow paint and their inhabitants made to wear yellow clothes. Denmark decreed the yellow flag in 1887 for their own ships to signify they were healthy and the practice was adopted internationally. In December 1347, the residents of Messina in Sicily watched bemused as 12 trading ships from Black Sea ports made desperately incompetent efforts to dock alongside the harbour walls. Then the reason became devastatingly clear; very few of their crews were still alive. The living were emaciated skeletons, covered with black boils that oozed blood and pus. The authorities ordered the ships out of the harbour but it was too late. Within five years, the Black Death would kill over 20 million people in Europe. It is believed that the source of infection on these ships originated in the Middle East and China. The symptoms were swellings of the groin, under the armpits with all the side effects of fever, aches and pains, diarrhoea and vomiting. This air borne bacillus brought a very nasty death to a staggering one third of the population. Not only people, but farm animals were infected which further spread the disease and brought food shortages even with a reduced population. In 1377 a system was established in Dubrovnik whereby ships were to wait for 30 days before entering port which was based on medical advice at the time. Then it appeared religion got involved by quoting all the significant traumas in the Bible were for 40 days and 40 nights. So this became the norm and as Dubrovnik was Venetian at the time and the language was Italian, you know what word we got from that. In the trading port of Venice itself, arriving ships that were suspected of carrying infection were required to send the captain ashore in a boat to the Health Magistrate’s office where he would be interviewed through a glass window. Even then they were aware that infection could be spread by air droplets. The captain had to show a health certificate from the port of loading that his crew, passengers and cargo were free of infection. If there was doubt, they would all be sent to a quarantine station for 40 days and the whole ship fumigated. An isolation hospital was established on the island of Santa Maria di Nazareth. Are you paying attention here? This was in 1423. Nearly 600 years ago. Interesting how the self-claimed leading authority on Coronavirus and just about everything else in the Western World (‘It will disappear like magic’) and whose daily reading attention span is reported to be almost the number of words on the average postage stamp, wants to call the infection the Wuhan Virus or China Virus because of its source of origin. Fine with me, but let’s call the Spanish flu by its correct name. Remember that from your extensive reading Mr. POTUS? You know, the one that infected a third of the world’s population and killed between 25 and 50 million people 100 years ago? Probably not, it wasn’t on Fox TV. It was called the Spanish flu by the Allies to focus on how devastating it was in that neutral country during the latter days of WW1. But mainly to hide the fact it was slaughtering millions in the rest of the world. So let’s call it by its correct name: The Kansas Flu. According to the Kansas Historical Society the first recorded case was a US Army cook at Camp Funston who was hospitalised with a 104˚F fever. It quickly spread through the Army base to infect 54,000 troops in one month. 1,100 were hospitalised and 38 died. A local doctor reported this to the Public Health Service but he got the same brushoff as the whistle blower in Wuhan who also became infected. Fortunately, he survived. So they were packed into troopships and freighters, (Humphrey Bogart was a crew member on one) and sent to the jammed mud and muck of the trenches of WW1. This deadly H1N1 influenza-A virus lasted two years and mutated to become the most deadly disease ever known on the face of the earth. Not just the first wave. It changed to a more virulent and deadly strain, killing people in less than a day. Mainly fit people between 25 and 35 years of age, who drowned in the fluid of their own lungs. The devastation of this virus was prolonged by governments not considering any social distancing, such as practiced by the Venetians, because it might affect production in the munitions factories and slow the big Liberty Loan Drives. 200,000 people gathered in Philadelphia in September 1918 for a Liberty Loan Drive after they were told the Flu had passed. Well, maybe the first wave had passed but two days later, every hospital bed was filled. A third wave developed in … Australia of all places and worked its way back to Europe. So let’s all follow the advice of people who know what they are talking about. Today, this is very unlikely to be the leader of a major world power.
  2. Sign Up to Afloat e-News Get up to date news to keep you afloat

评论（默认中文）

theendisney 2026/04/05 05:57
令人着迷的是，航空公司尽管几乎没有乘客，但仍继续飞往埃博拉病毒区。

即使所有座位都满了，他们也无法获得无法避免的惊天动地的利润。经济不需要这样做。风险收益比超出图表。

做出这样决定的人真的应该入狱吗？我想不出更好的杀人方式。只要派出一些瘟疫船就可以很快完成任务。

It was facinating to see airlines continue to fly into ebola zones eventho they barely had passengers.

Even if all seats are full they dont make earth shaking profit that cant be avoided. The economy doesnt require doing it. The risk benefit ratio is off the chart.

People making such decisions should really be behind bars? I cant think of a better way to kill. Just send some plague ships and it will make short work.

在 HN 中查看
decimalenough 2026/04/05 06:19
伦敦希思罗机场等机场的航班时刻是备受争议的商品，价格高达数百万美元，并附有“使用它或失去它”的规则。如果您暂时停止飞行某条航线，您可能会永久失去该航线。
Slots at airports like London Heathrow are hotly contested commodities with price tags in the millions and come with "use it or lose it" rules attached. If you stop flying a route temporarily, you may lose it permanently.
在 HN 中查看
theendisney 2026/04/05 12:30
这就像飞行员如果不遵守命令就会丢掉工作一样。我们可以把整个指挥系统关进监狱。包括那些因定期不飞往那里而惩罚他们的人。
That is like the pilot losing his job if he doesn't follow orders. We can put the entire chain of command in prison. Including those who punish them for periodically not flying there.
在 HN 中查看
bryanrasmussen 2026/04/05 12:36
除非有相关法律，否则你不能将某人关进监狱，应该修改法律，以便在这种情况下取​​消航班而不损害航空公司。
you can't put someone in prison unless you have a relevant law, the law should be amended so that in such cases the flights should be canceled without damaging the airline.

mr_00ff00 2026/04/05 08:25
> 来自黑海港口的 12 艘商船靠港墙靠岸的努力极其无能。然后原因就变得非常清楚了。他们的船员很少还活着。活着的人都是瘦骨嶙峋的骷髅，浑身长满黑色脓疮，渗着血和脓。

无法想象在中世纪的意大利目睹这会是什么样子。

> 12 trading ships from Black Sea ports made desperately incompetent efforts to dock alongside the harbour walls. Then the reason became devastatingly clear; very few of their crews were still alive. The living were emaciated skeletons, covered with black boils that oozed blood and pus.

Can’t imagine what that must have been like to witness in medieval Italy.

在 HN 中查看
ETH_start 2026/04/05 03:25
关于西班牙（或堪萨斯）流感，有一些证据表明第二波比第一波致命得多，因为与第一次世界大战有关的不寻常做法：

感染毒力较强菌株的士兵更有可能被送往军队医院，而感染毒力较弱菌株的士兵则更有可能留在战壕中。

军队医院是比战壕海湾更活跃的传播媒介，因此正常的传播模式被颠倒了，毒性更强的菌株比毒性较小的菌株传播得更快。

在正常情况下，病情严重的人会呆在家里，而病情较轻的人会去上班，这往往会随着时间的推移，促使高毒力病毒的毒力逐渐减弱。

Regarding the Spanish (or Kansas) flu, there is some evidence suggesting that the second wave was much deadlier than the first because of an unusual practice connected to World War I:

Soldiers infected with more virulent strains were more likely to be shipped to military hospitals, while those infected with less virulent strains were more likely to remain in the trenches.

The military hospitals were much more active vectors of transmission than the bays of the trenches, so the normal pattern of transmission was inverted, with the more virulent strains spreading faster than the less virulent ones.

Under normal conditions, the very sick would stay home while the less sick would go to work, which would tend to push highly virulent viruses toward becoming less virulent over time.

在 HN 中查看
jmalicki 2026/04/05 08:36
这让我想起一个事实：医院获得性肺炎是美国 ICU 患者死亡的主要原因。

在即将死亡的人中，最致命的是机会性细菌性肺炎，这种肺炎甚至在医院或其他受损患者之外几乎不存在。

This reminds me of the fact that hospital-acquired pneumonia is the leading cause of death in ICUs in the US.

Among people who are already going to die, the thing that kills them the most is an opportunistic bacterial pneumonia that barely even exists outside of hospitals, or other compromised patients.

SoftTalker 2026/04/05 09:45
是的，当你生病的时候，你真的不想住院。这是一位医生朋友告诉我的。
Yeah you really don't want to be in a hospital when you're sick. A doctor friend told me this.
